protocol.name: wlr_screencopy_unstable_v1
protocol.copyright:
Copyright © 2018 Simon Ser Copyright © 2019 Andri Yngvason
Permission to use, copy, modify, distribute, and sell this software and its documentation for any purpose is hereby granted without fee, provided that the above copyright notice appear in all copies and that both that copyright notice and this permission notice appear in supporting documentation, and that the name of the copyright holders not be used in advertising or publicity pertaining to distribution of the software without specific, written prior permission. The copyright holders make no representations about the suitability of this software for any purpose. It is provided "as is" without express or implied warranty.

description.summary: screen content capturing on client buffers

protocol.description: This protocol allows clients to ask the compositor to copy part of the screen content to a client buffer.

interface.name: zwlr_screencopy_manager_v1

description.summary: manager to inform clients and begin capturing

interface.description: This object is a manager which offers requests to start capturing from a source.

request.name: capture_output
request.description: Capture the next frame of an entire output.

request.name: capture_output_region
request.description:
Capture the next frame of an output's region.
The region is given in output logical coordinates, see xdg_output.logical_size. The region will be clamped to the output's extents.

request.name: destroy
request.description: All objects created by the manager will still remain valid, until their appropriate destroy request has been called.

interface.name: zwlr_screencopy_frame_v1
interface.description:
This object represents a single frame.
When created, a series of buffer events will be sent, each representing a supported buffer type. The "buffer_done" event is sent afterwards to indicate that all supported buffer types have been enumerated. The client will then be able to send a "copy" request. If the capture is successful, the compositor will send a "flags" followed by a "ready" event.

event.name: buffer
event.description: Provides information about wl_shm buffer parameters that need to be used for this frame. This event is sent once after the frame is created if wl_shm buffers are supported.

request.name: copy
request.description: Copy the frame to the supplied buffer. The buffer must have a the correct size, see zwlr_screencopy_frame_v1.buffer and zwlr_screencopy_frame_v1.buffer_done. The buffer needs to have a supported format.

event.name: flags
event.description: Provides flags about the frame. This event is sent once before the "ready" event.

event.name: ready

description.summary: indicates frame is available for reading

event.description: Called as soon as the frame is copied, indicating it is available for reading. This event includes the time at which the presentation took place.

event.name: failed
event.description: This event indicates that the attempted frame copy has failed.

request.name: destroy

description.summary: delete this object, used or not

request.description: Destroys the frame. This request can be sent at any time by the client.
